issues.title: CopilotPanel chat has no ARIA roles, live region, or keyboard navigation
issues.description: The chat message list has no role='log' or aria-live='polite', messages lack role='listitem', the input lacks aria-label, and there is no keyboard shortcut to open/close the panel. Screen readers cannot follow the conversation.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the PanelMessageRenderer and the main CopilotPanel component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The message scroll container is a plain div with no ARIA attributes, the AgentMentionInput wrapper has no aria-label, and the panel has no landmark role. AFTER: (1) Add role='log' and aria-live='polite' to the messages scroll container div. (2) Wrap each message in a div with role='listitem'. (3) Add aria-label='AI Copilot conversation' to the messages container. (4) Add aria-label='AI Copilot' and role='complementary' to the outermost panel aside/div. (5) Add an onKeyDown handler to the panel that closes it on Escape key press by calling the store's closeCopilot. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: CopilotPanel shows no error state when AI message fails
issues.description: If useMultiAgentChat returns an error or a message send fails, there is no visible error state, no retry button, and no toast notification. The user sees nothing happen. The acceptance criteria require toast + inline retry on network failures.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the main CopilotPanel component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component destructures from useMultiAgentChat but does not check for or display an error state. If sendMessage throws, the UI silently does nothing. AFTER: (1) Destructure 'error' from useMultiAgentChat. (2) When error is truthy, render an inline error banner at the bottom of the messages area with the text 'Unable to get a response. Check your connection.' and a 'Retry' button that re-sends the last user message. Style the banner with backgroundColor: colors.statusCriticalSubtle, borderLeft: 3px solid colors.statusCritical, padding spacing['3'], borderRadius borderRadius.md. (3) Import and call toast.error('AI response failed') from sonner when error first becomes truthy using a useEffect. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: CopilotPanel is fixed 480px wide with no mobile responsive layout
issues.description: The slide-in panel uses a fixed width of ~480px. On mobile (<768px) it should be full-screen with a bottom-sheet style. Touch targets on suggested prompt buttons are smaller than the 44px minimum required by the design standards.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the main CopilotPanel component where the panel container div is styled, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The panel has a fixed width (e.g., width: '480px' or similar) and maxWidth: '85vw', which still leaves it as a side panel on small screens. Suggested prompt buttons have small padding. AFTER: (1) Change the width style to use a CSS calc or conditional: on screens <= 768px use width: '100vw' and position the panel to cover the full screen (top:0, left:0, right:0, bottom:0). Use a media query check via window.innerWidth or a useMediaQuery hook (if available) to conditionally set fullscreen. If no hook exists, set maxWidth to '100vw' and width to 'min(480px, 100vw)'. (2) Ensure all suggested prompt buttons have minHeight: '44px' and padding at least spacing['3'] vertically. (3) On mobile, move the input area to a fixed bottom position so the keyboard does not push it off screen. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AI insights endpoint returns empty array when aiService is unconfigured and DB has no seed data
issues.description: getAiInsights falls through to a Supabase query on ai_insights table which is likely empty. There are no fallback mock insights for the empty state. The dashboard AI panel and FloatingAIButton show 0 insights with no helpful empty state guidance.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/ai.ts, in the getAiInsights function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: When aiService is not configured and the Supabase ai_insights table returns empty data, the function returns { insights: [...budgetInsights], dataSource: 'ai-cached' } which may be an empty array, giving users no AI value. AFTER: After the Supabase query, if both (data || []).length === 0 and budgetInsights.length === 0, generate 3-5 hardcoded construction-relevant starter insights as fallback. Example insights: { id: 'fallback-1', type: 'schedule_risk', severity: 'info', title: 'Schedule Analysis Available', description: 'Add schedule phases to enable AI delay prediction and critical path analysis.', affectedEntities: [], suggestedAction: 'Navigate to Schedule to add project phases', confidence: 1, source: 'fallback' as const, createdAt: new Date().toISOString(), generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(), expiresAt: null, dismissed: false }. Include similar ones for budget setup, RFI tracking, and safety configuration. Set dataSource to 'ai-fallback' as const (add this to the AiInsightsResponse type if needed in the types file). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: FloatingAIButton has no loading/skeleton state and no empty state tooltip
issues.description: The button renders immediately with insightCount 0 while useAIInsights is loading. There is no skeleton/pulse animation during load, no tooltip explaining what the button does, and clicking while loading may open an empty copilot panel.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component destructures { data: insights } from useAIInsights but does not check isLoading or isError. When loading, the badge shows 0 or nothing. There is no tooltip. AFTER: (1) Destructure { data: insights, isLoading, isError } from useAIInsights. (2) While isLoading, add a pulse animation to the button (animation: 'pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite' via inline style keyframes or opacity oscillation). (3) Add a title attribute to the button: when isLoading show 'Loading AI insights...', when insights exist show `${insightCount} AI insight${insightCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''} available`, otherwise show 'Open AI Copilot'. (4) Add aria-label matching the title text. (5) If isError, change the badge to show a small warning icon (AlertTriangle from lucide-react, size 10) instead of a count, colored colors.statusPending. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AgentMessage uses hardcoded hex colors instead of theme tokens for gradient
issues.description: The coordinator message avatar uses `colors.indigo` in a gradient which may not exist as a theme token, and several inline styles use string interpolation that could reference non-existent tokens. The design standards require no hardcoded colors.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/AgentMessage.tsx, in the coordinator message rendering branch where the avatar gradient is defined, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The background style uses `linear-gradient(135deg, ${colors.statusReview} 0%, ${colors.indigo} 100%)`. The token colors.indigo may not exist in the theme, which would render as 'undefined' in the gradient string. AFTER: Replace `colors.indigo` with `colors.statusReview` to create a monochromatic gradient, or use a valid theme token like colors.statusInfo for a purple-to-blue effect: `linear-gradient(135deg, ${colors.statusReview} 0%, ${colors.statusInfo} 100%)`. Verify that all color references in this file use tokens from the imported colors object and none reference tokens that don't exist. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: CopilotPanel has no real-time subscription for AI insights updates
issues.description: When another user or a background AI process creates new insights, the copilot panel does not update. The acceptance criteria require real-time updates within 2 seconds via Supabase Realtime subscription.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the main CopilotPanel component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component does not subscribe to any Supabase Realtime channel for ai_insights or ai_messages table changes. New insights from background processes are not reflected until manual refresh. AFTER: Add a useEffect that subscribes to Supabase Realtime on the ai_messages table filtered by the current conversation_id (if one exists). When a new INSERT event is received with role='assistant', append the message to the local messages state or invalidate the query cache. Use supabase.channel(`copilot:${conversationId}`).on('postgres_changes', { event: 'INSERT', schema: 'public', table: 'ai_messages', filter: `conversation_id=eq.${conversationId}` }, callback).subscribe(). Return an unsubscribe cleanup function. If conversationId is null, skip subscription. Import supabase from '../../lib/supabase'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AGENT_COLORS exported but also redefined locally in AgentMessage.tsx
issues.description: AGENT_COLORS is defined as a const in AgentMessage.tsx and also exported from it, but CopilotPanel.tsx imports { AGENT_COLORS } from './AgentMessage'. The type Record<AgentDomain, {fg,bg,subtle}> is duplicated. This creates maintenance risk.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/AgentMessage.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: AGENT_COLORS is defined as a module-level const and exported alongside the AgentMessage component. The CopilotPanel imports it from this file. This couples presentation constants to a component file. AFTER: Add the 'export' keyword to AGENT_COLORS if it is not already exported (verify the current export). Ensure the type annotation is explicit: export const AGENT_COLORS: Record<AgentDomain, { fg: string; bg: string; subtle: string }> = { ... }. This is a verification fix — confirm the export exists and the type is correct. If AGENT_COLORS is already properly exported and typed, no changes needed. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: SuggestedPrompts re-renders on every parent render due to non-memoized prompts array
issues.description: The pageContextPrompts object is defined at module level (good), but the component receives prompts as a prop which may be a new array reference each render from the parent, causing unnecessary re-renders of all prompt buttons.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/SuggestedPrompts.tsx, in the SuggestedPrompts component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component is a plain React.FC that re-renders whenever its parent re-renders because it is not memoized. The prompts prop is an array that may be a new reference each time. AFTER: (1) Wrap the component with React.memo: export const SuggestedPrompts: React.FC<SuggestedPromptsProps> = React.memo(({ prompts, onSelect }) => { ... }). (2) Add a displayName: SuggestedPrompts.displayName = 'SuggestedPrompts'. This prevents re-renders when the parent updates but prompts and onSelect haven't changed. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: AI copilot sends user messages without sanitizing potential injection content
issues.description: User input is sent directly to the AI edge function without any client-side sanitization or length validation. While server-side validation should exist, the client should enforce a max message length and strip HTML to prevent XSS in rendered responses.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx, in the handleSend or equivalent submit function (the callback that calls onSend), make these specific changes: BEFORE: The value from the textarea is passed directly to onSend(value) without any validation beyond checking if it's empty. AFTER: (1) Add a MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH constant of 4000 characters at the top of the file. (2) Before calling onSend, trim the value and check if value.trim().length > MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH. If so, show a toast notification: toast.error(`Message too long. Maximum ${MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH} characters.`) and return without sending. Import toast from 'sonner'. (3) Strip any HTML tags from the input using a simple regex: const sanitized = value.trim().replace(/<[^>]*>/g, ''). Pass sanitized to onSend. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: The multi-agent architecture with @mentions (schedule, cost, safety, quality, compliance, document agents) and generative UI blocks is a significant differentiator over Procore's bolt-on Procore Assist chatbot and Autodesk's Construction IQ. Procore's AI answers questions but cannot render interactive data tables, approval cards, or cost breakdowns inline. The BatchActionPreview and human-in-the-loop approval pattern exceed anything shipping in construction PM today. However, without proper error handling, accessibility, and mobile responsiveness, this sophisticated architecture is unreachable by the superintendent in the field — which is the exact user the product vision prioritizes.
summary: The Project Intelligence module has an impressively ambitious multi-agent AI architecture with generative UI rendering, specialist agent routing via @mentions, and rich interactive components (data tables, charts, approval cards, cost breakdowns, safety alerts). This is genuinely differentiated from competitors. However, five critical gaps undermine its usability: (1) zero accessibility — no ARIA roles, live regions, or keyboard navigation makes the copilot unusable for screen readers and violates WCAG AA requirements; (2) no error handling when AI calls fail — users see silent failures with no retry path; (3) no mobile-responsive layout — the panel is fixed-width and unusable on field devices which contradicts the field-first mandate; (4) empty data state produces a blank experience with no fallback insights to demonstrate AI value; and (5) the floating AI button lacks loading states, tooltips, and aria-labels. The underlying component library (generative UI renderers, agent stream panel, batch action preview) is well-structured with proper React.memo usage and TypeScript typing, but the integration layer connecting these to real user workflows has significant gaps in error resilience, accessibility, and responsive design.
